description: You are a transport economist at the Italian Ministry of Infrastructure and Sustainable Mobility (MIMS), evaluating a cordon-based congestion pricing policy for Bologna's Pasubio corridor using the SUMO scenario at /home/ga/SUMO_Scenarios/bologna_pasubio/. Conduct a baseline simulation to extract performance metrics (vehicle count, VKT, average duration, average speed, time loss, and fuel consumption) and save them to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/baseline_traffic_economics.csv with columns: metric, value, unit. Model the congestion pricing effect by creating a modified route file (pasubio_priced.rou.xml) and configuration (run_priced.sumocfg) that reduces private vehicle trips through a core corridor charging zone of at least 5 edges by 15-25%, ensuring public transit is exempt. Execute the pricing simulation and save the corresponding metrics to /home/ga/SUMO_Output/priced_traffic_economics.csv in the same format. Produce a cost-benefit analysis at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/congestion_pricing_cba.csv with columns: category, baseline_value, priced_value, change_pct, annual_monetized_eur. Use economic values of 15 EUR/hour for travel time, 1.80 EUR/liter for fuel, and 100 EUR/tonne for CO2 to calculate travel time savings, fuel savings, emission reductions, and revenue. Finally, document the policy design, demand impacts, and implementation recommendations in an executive brief at /home/ga/SUMO_Output/congestion_pricing_brief.txt.
